mission.title: opentelemetry-operator: [collector] Specify the collector's configuration with structure yaml instead of a string
mission.description: [collector] Specify the collector's configuration with structure yaml instead of a string. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current opentelemetry-operator deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opentelemetry-operator version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
helm list -n opentelemetry-operator 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opentelemetry-operator installation.

steps.title: Review opentelemetry-operator configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentelemetry-operator configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
kubectl get configmap -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentelemetry-operator
```
Currently the only way to define the collector's config in the `OpentlemetryCollector` resource is via a string in the `Spec.Config` field.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [collector] Specify the collector's configuration with…
steps.description:
I'm not quite sure I understand the outcome of this discussion. But here is my take :snail: 

We can overwrite the [collector's internal config structure](https://pkg.go.dev/go.opentelemetry.io/collector.76.1/otelcol#Config) Unmarshal method and support a native yaml configuration as well as parsing a single string into the same structure. This should be done during the validation process in the webhook.
To avoid rewriting logic on everyplace the current string field is used, we might add a

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
kubectl get events -n opentelemetry-operator --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[collector] Specify the collector's configuration with…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I'm not quite sure I understand the outcome of this discussion. But here is my take :snail: 

We can overwrite the [collector's internal config structure](https://pkg.go.dev/go.opentelemetry.io/collector.76.1/otelcol#Config) Unmarshal method and support a native yaml configuration as well as parsing a single string into the same structure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentelemetry-operator installed or the issue environment reproducible.
